sanskrit: इदं ते नातपस्काय नाभक्ताय कदाचन । न चाशुश्रूषवे वाच्यं न च मां योऽभ्यसूयति ॥
hindiTranslation: यह सर्वगुह्यतम वचन अतपस्वीको मत कहना अभक्तको कभी मत कहना जो सुनना नहीं चाहता, उसको मत कहना और जो मेरेमें दोषदृष्टि करता है, उससे भी मत कहना ॥
englishTranslation: Never speak this to one without discipline, one without devotion, one unwilling to listen, or one who finds fault with me.
essence: Deep truth must be shared only with those ready to receive it.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Keep this deepest teaching away from people who are unreceptive, undisciplined, or hostile to Krishna.
simpleInsight: Sacred truth needs the right listener. Not every ear is ready for what can transform it.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Krishna has finished his final teaching to Arjuna. After revealing surrender as the way to freedom, he now guards that teaching: it should not be given to the undisciplined, the unreceptive, the unwilling, or the hostile.
modernRelevance: You finally say something honest and life-changing, but the room is wrong for it. Some people want a debate, not transformation; some ears are simply not open yet.
translationLiteral: इदम् (this) / ते (to you) / न (not) / अतपस्काय (to one without discipline) / न (not) / अभक्ताय (to one without devotion) / कदाचन (ever) / न (not) / च (and) / अशुश्रूषवे (to one unwilling to listen) / वाच्यम् (to be spoken) / न (not) / च (and) / माम् (me) / यः (who) / अभ्यसूयति (finds fault with)
